item.title: Anthropic scrambles after Trump administration freezes its top AI models
item.description: Export controls on Fable and Mythos raise doubts over how US will police the most powerful AI systems

item.title: Strait of Hormuz backlog could take weeks to clear after US-Iran deal
item.description: Analysts warn oil flows may be slow to recover and remain vulnerable to renewed disruption

item.title: UK consumer losses as a result of financial fraud hit 4-year high in 2025
item.description: Figures will intensify pressure from banks for government to force tech groups to crack down on fraudulent activity

item.title: Badenoch courts City with call for greater risk-taking
item.description: UK opposition leader seeks to reset relations after market turbulence under ex-prime minister Liz Truss in 2022

item.title: Macron to pull out the stops to keep Trump at G7 gathering
item.description: First rich nations summit since Iran war launched, a make-or-break British by-election, Warsh chairs his first FOMC meeting

item.title: Starmer to announce Australia-style social media ban for teenagers
item.description: PM set to announce that he will ban younger teenagers from some social media platforms and curb their daily use

item.title: Keir Starmer poised to weaken electric vehicle targets
item.description: Prime minister to reduce all-electric cap from 80% to 50% by end of the decade amid fears of job losses

item.title: ‘Can a machine do this job?’ is the wrong question
item.description: By shifting work to the consumer, AI will usher in a self-service economy

item.title: Electrification jumps up corporate agenda after energy crisis, survey finds
item.description: Security and cost concerns elevate business push for overhaul of power systems

item.title: UK intercepts Russian shadow fleet oil tanker in English Channel
item.description: Prime minister hails ‘yet another blow to Russia’ as part of effort to clamp down on those fuelling Ukraine war

item.title: Wall Street digests record fundraising haul as AI race intensifies
item.description: SpaceX, Anthropic and Alphabet financings highlight investors’ willingness to absorb a torrent of new issuance

item.title: Bridgepoint weighs $1bn sale of anti-drone group guarding World Cup sites
item.description: Danish company MyDefence has already drawn buyout and defence sector interest as demand surges for counter-UAV kit

item.title: Germany seeks Deutsche Börse exemption from EU supervision
item.description: Stock exchange would have option to remain under domestic authority as part of new EU markets regime

item.title: Defence tech start-up Anduril calls for reset of US arms export controls
item.description: CEO of California-based group wants to enlist allies in helping with production of lower-cost weapons

item.title: Tabletop game titans offer two ways to play the nerd economy
item.description: While Games Workshop investors have tripled their money, Hasbro shareholders have seen barely any increase

item.title: Funding Circle claims two-thirds of payouts under SME loan scheme
item.description: Lender used successor to Covid-era small business loan scheme to hand out loans to higher-risk borrowers

item.title: DR Congo pushes Belgium to release millions of colonial-era documents
item.description: Congolese minister met Belgian officials to discuss records mapping former colony’s mineral wealth

item.title: Fusion industry suppliers bet on race for reactors creating a $73bn market
item.description: Groups eye windfall as technology’s potential to deliver abundant power attracts investors

item.title: Overseas buyers push UK dealmaking to two-decade high
item.description: Value of UK deals by foreign buyers more than triples compared with the same period last year

item.title: The cruise ship CEO on how to run cities at sea
item.description: Jason Liberty steered Royal Caribbean’s pandemic recovery but faces tough new challenges

item.title: How will history judge today’s chief executives?
item.description: Leading through the AI storm is shaping up to be a test for the ages

item.title: China tees up digital payments system to compete with dollar
item.description: Beijing’s cross-border currency platform will be backed by central banks of Hong Kong, Thailand, UAE and Saudi Arabia

item.title: ‘Everything is too high-tech’: Gen Z discovers digital cameras
item.description: Smartphone images can look ‘artificial’, says Fujifilm executive, as runaway price rises threaten sales boom

item.title: Investment firms join Donald Trump’s $100bn race for Venezuelan oil
item.description: Lionheart Capital and Keo Energy create Nasdaq-listed company to pursue assets in Latin American nation.

item.title: Europe’s fighter jet fiasco
item.description: The collapse of Paris and Berlin’s joint venture raises the question of whether either country can do defence alone

item.title: How Wall Street pulled off the biggest IPO in history for SpaceX
item.description: Bankers convinced investors to believe in a sci-fi strategy, overlook steep losses and hand full control to Elon Musk

item.title: UK housebuilder Vistry offers staff voluntary redundancy
item.description: Company is trying to preserve cash to shore up its business amid market slowdown

item.title: The sweet smell of World Cup dollars
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: a dispatch from LA and Mercedes F1’s robust finances

item.title: Goldman and JPMorgan ease office working rules to counter World Cup disruption
item.description: Wall Street lenders say employees can ask to work from home on match days to ease congestion in host cities

item.title: Frasers is better at making headlines than creating shareholder value
item.description: Mike Ashley’s penchant for stake-building is not that well suited to the public markets
